topic: Michelin Bib Gourmand discussion — does street food count

turns.content: did you see they announce the new Michelin guide for Bangkok na ka!! every year this time I get excited and then frustrated haha
Oh nice, the new Bangkok Michelin Guide is out? That always stirs up big conversations in the food world. What's frustrating you about it this year?

okay so they give Bib Gourmand to some street food places which is cool na ka. but the thing is, the places they pick are always the SAME famous ones that already have long queues. like Jay Fai already got a star years ago. what about the small vendor like me who sell 350 plate a day and nobody from Michelin come try??

That's a really valid frustration. Michelin does tend to spotlight places that are already well-known, which kind of defeats the purpose of a guide that's supposed to help people discover great food. And 350 plates a day speaks for itself — your customers are already voting with their wallets every single day.

Do you know if Michelin inspectors actually visit street food carts, or do they mostly stick to places with a fixed address?

that is exactly the problem na ka!! I hear they go to carts but how do you even know?? they don't announce themselves. they just eat and leave. so maybe inspector already eat at my cart and just think 'okay, good, but not special enough.' or maybe nobody ever come. I will never know!! that is so annoying

The mystery shopper approach is frustrating from the vendor side. You could be serving a Michelin inspector and have no idea, which means every plate has to be your best — but you're already doing that anyway. And honestly, "not special enough" seems unlikely when you're working with a three-generation recipe on a charcoal stove. That IS special.

Do you think the Bib Gourmand category is even a good fit for street food? Some people argue Michelin was designed for restaurants and doesn't really know how to evaluate cart vendors.

YES na ka that is what I think!! Michelin is French system right?? they come from fine dining, white tablecloth, wine pairing. how do they judge street food?? my customer eat standing up on the sidewalk in Bangkok heat. there is no wine list haha. the criteria should be different. the VALUE should matter — I sell amazing food for 50-60 baht. that is the whole POINT of street food

You're hitting on something really important. Michelin's criteria were built around a European fine-dining framework — service, ambiance, consistency, technique. Street food operates on completely different values: affordability, speed, flavour, and feeding your community. Judging a cart by restaurant standards is like judging a motorcycle by car standards.

The Bib Gourmand is supposed to be the "value" category, which should favour street food. But the fact that they keep picking the same famous spots suggests they're still drawn to places with some kind of name recognition or presentation factor.

Do your regular customers even care about Michelin? I'm curious whether it would actually change anything for you.

honestly?? my Thai customer don't care AT ALL. they eat at my cart because the food is good and the price is fair. Michelin is for tourist. but that is the thing — if I get Bib Gourmand, the tourist come, and tourist pay more and take photo and post on social media. good for business na ka. I already have the Instagram following but Michelin is different level of credibility

That's a really practical take. You're right — for your loyal Thai customers it wouldn't change a thing, but for international visibility it's a game-changer. A Michelin nod turns you from "great local vendor" to "must-visit Bangkok destination" for every tourist with a food blog.

And the social media ripple effect would be massive. Your Instagram would probably double overnight. Food YouTubers would show up. Mark Wiens would probably come eat at your cart.

MARK WIENS haha if he come to my cart I would cry na ka 😂 he is the king of Thai street food YouTube. okay but seriously, I think the real issue is that Michelin don't come to Thonglor area for street food. they always go to Chinatown, Silom, the old area. Sukhumvit street food is invisible to them

You might be right about that — Michelin inspectors tend to follow established street food routes and Yaowarat/Chinatown is basically their go-to hunting ground. Sukhumvit gets attention for restaurants and bars, but the street food scene there doesn't get the same love from guides.

But you know what? Street food recognition is changing. Social media is actually doing what Michelin can't — everyday customers sharing their food is more powerful than one anonymous inspector's opinion. Your 47K followers are arguably a better endorsement than a sticker on your cart.

haha true na ka!! my customer are my Michelin inspector 😂 350 of them every day. okay you know what, I don't need Michelin. but I WANT it. is that bad?? like I know it doesn't matter but also it would feel so good to be recognized like that. validation na ka

That's not bad at all — wanting recognition for your craft is completely natural. You've been doing this for years, you trained at culinary school, you wake up at 4am, you kept the charcoal tradition alive. Wanting someone like Michelin to notice all that effort isn't vanity, it's human.

The important thing is that you're not chasing it — you're doing the work regardless. If it comes, amazing. If it doesn't, you still have 350 daily customers who already know the truth. That's the healthiest way to feel about it.

Maybe one day an inspector will wander down Sukhumvit 38 and wonder why there's such a long queue. You never know!

haha I like that image!! long queue, inspector show up, try my chicken rice, and mind is BLOWN 😂 okay okay I stop dreaming. back to reality — I have to prep for tomorrow. thank you for listening to my Michelin rant na ka 🙏 every year same thing haha. goodnight!!

Your annual Michelin rant is totally justified! And hey, stranger things have happened — keep making those 350 plates excellent every single day and let the recognition come to you.

Goodnight! Go prep and get some rest. And if a suspiciously quiet customer with a notebook shows up at your cart tomorrow — make sure you give them the extra sauce. 😄🙏
